rationale: Load-bearing hooks for the runtime 'priced-or-it-doesnt-ship' serving-admission gate (docs/approved/priced-or-it-doesnt-ship.md, issue #1016 v1). The gate is a thin per-route injection (one guarded call + early return) into 6 upstream-shaped service forwarders; each touch is small and compiles clean if dropped, so an upstream merge or refactor can silently revert it and re-open the catch-all $0-billing hole. These sentinels pin the gate companion, its billing-oracle predicate, the degraded-source fail-open, the DI wiring, every per-route hook (on billing's exact key), the setting constant, and the enabled-set getter so preflight + the upstream-merge PR shape check fail loudly if any disappears.

sentinels.rationale: The gate core. R3 consistency (design §7) is now BY CONSTRUCTION: the gate judges via billing's own oracle — `resolve(billingModel)` is BillingService.GetModelPricing, rejecting iff it returns ErrModelPricingUnavailable, the SAME call+key billing uses to decide $0. No catalog shadow predicate exists to drift (an earlier draft's tkIsModelEffectivelyPriced was removed). tkPricingSystemDegraded + the canary make the gate fail-OPEN when the whole pricing source is degraded (else one bad pricing file 404s 100% of enabled-platform traffic). The literal error envelope strings (not_found_error / invalid_request_error + model_not_priced subcode) are the byte-aligned shapes clients depend on. NotifyPricingMissing is v1's pricing channel (reject → Feishu card → ops hot-fixes the price) satisfying R4.

must_contain: func (s *GeminiMessagesCompatService) SetPricedServingGateDeps(catalog *PricingCatalogService, billing *BillingService, setting *SettingService, notifier PricingMissingNotifier)
sentinels.rationale: Post-construction DI setters and the per-service one-liner wrappers each route hook calls. SetPricedServingGateDeps injects the BillingService (the gate's oracle) alongside setting/catalog/notifier — the 4-arg shape proves billing is wired (drop it and the gate dead-codes silently off in prod while unit tests pass).

must_contain: if !s.tkPricedServingGate(ctx, c, tkGateWireOpenAI, account.Platform, billingModel, originalModel) {
sentinels.rationale: Route hook 1/6 — OpenAI native /v1/responses (+chat). Judges billingModel (the mapped id openai native bills on) — its exact billing key. Before upstream forward / SSE stream start. A silent revert re-opens $0 serving for unpriced OpenAI models when openai joins the enabled set.

must_contain: if !s.tkPricedServingGate(ctx, c, tkGateWireOpenAI, account.Platform, originalModel, originalModel) {
sentinels.rationale: Route hook 2/6 — responses-bridge (ForwardAsResponses), OpenAI wire envelope. Judges originalModel (billing's key on this path), before forward / stream start.

must_contain: if !s.tkPricedServingGate(ctx, c, tkGateWireOpenAI, account.Platform, originalModel, originalModel) {
sentinels.rationale: Route hook 3/6 — chat-completions-bridge (ForwardAsChatCompletions), OpenAI wire envelope. Judges originalModel, before forward / stream start.

must_contain: if !s.tkPricedServingGate(ctx, c, tkGateWireAnthropic, account.Platform, originalModel, originalModel) {
if !s.tkPricedServingGate(ctx, c, tkGateWireGemini, account.Platform, originalModel, originalModel) {
sentinels.rationale: Route hooks 4/6 + 5/6 — gemini compat Forward (Anthropic /v1/messages ingress → tkGateWireAnthropic envelope) AND ForwardNative (native Gemini ingress → tkGateWireGemini envelope; countTokens is exempted just before this call). BOTH judge originalModel (billing's key here — gemini native bills result.Model=originalModel; judging mappedModel was BLOCKER1, the catch-all leak). This is the first-launch enabled platform, so a revert here is the highest-impact. The tkBillingService struct-field anchor proves the gate's oracle dep slot survives (the gemini compat service holds none of its deps on the upstream constructor).

must_contain: if !s.tkPricedServingGate(ctx, c, tkGateWireOpenAI, account.Platform, originalModel, originalModel) {
sentinels.rationale: Route hook 6/6 — the third gemini ingress (OpenAI /v1/chat/completions onto a gemini account, forwardClaudeBodyAsChatCompletions). This route had NO gate in v1's first cut (BLOCKER2) — an OpenAI-CC client could hit a gemini account with an unpriced model and serve $0 on the launch platform. OpenAI wire envelope; judges originalModel.

must_contain: if !s.tkPricedServingGate(ctx, c, tkGateWireAnthropic, account.Platform, originalModel, originalModel) {
sentinels.rationale: Route hook (native anthropic /v1/messages catch-all). Inserted right after the deprecated-model gate (post-mapping, pre-forward) — the native catch-all (empty model_mapping = allow-all) is the original vulnerability hot spot. anthropic is NOT in the first-launch enabled set but joins per the rollout plan; the hook (on originalModel, billing's key) must survive until then.

The setting key for the per-platform enabled SET. Renaming/removing it silently disables every IsPricedServingGateEnabled lookup (gate off for all platforms) without a compile error if a stale string literal lingers elsewhere.

must_contain: func (s *SettingService) IsPricedServingGateEnabled(ctx context.Context, platform string) bool
sentinels.rationale: The enabled-set getter (comma-separated SET membership, fail-open-toward-off) and the first-launch default (gemini/Vertex, D2). Losing the getter breaks the gate's enable check; losing the cold-start default means a fresh DB ships with the gate OFF, regressing the 'gemini/Vertex one-step ON' launch contract.
